steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: To the left of the blank: “ratified in 1804” is a participial phrase. To the right: “separated the elections...” is the main clause. An opening comma already marks where the phrase begins.

steps.title: Apply the rule for paired punctuation
steps.content: When a parenthetical phrase begins with a comma, it must end with one to maintain symmetry. The opening comma after “amendment” requires a closing comma after “1804.” Choices A, C, and D violate this—a colon doesn't apply, no punctuation leaves the phrase unclosed, and an em dash doesn't match the opening comma. That matches choice B — B is correct.
